Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session 293810eb-4c0e-4e57-b5e5-5b3a2c98700a.

Resolves #10081
Related: #10030

Adds durable Knowledge Base query telemetry and an Agent FAQ read model for the Concept Ontology / Golden Path loop. KB MCP calls are logged into shared SQLite `kb_query_log`, repeated `ask_knowledge_base` and `query_documents` questions are materialized into `kb_query_faqs`, the `list_agent_faqs` MCP surface exposes the read model, and `GapInferenceEngine` now emits `[KB_DEMAND_GAP]` when repeated agent questions map to concepts without strong guide coverage.

## Deltas from ticket

The first clustering pass is intentionally conservative: exact normalized-query grouping is used as the baseline, while `kbFaqSimilarityThreshold` is stored on FAQ rows as the calibration marker for later embedding-backed clustering once real traffic exists. This avoids false semantic merges before we have production query distribution data.

## Config Template Change

Changed `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.template.mjs` keys:
- `memoryCoreDbPath`
- `kbFaqMinCount`
- `kbFaqSimilarityThreshold`
- `kbFaqConceptLimit`

Local follow-up after merge: each active clone should update its gitignored `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` with these keys or recopy/apply the template defaults. Without `memoryCoreDbPath`, KB query telemetry is disabled with a warning instead of crashing.

Harness restart: recommended for Knowledge Base MCP and any daemon process that imports `GapInferenceEngine` / `GoldenPathSynthesizer`, so the new tool mapping and config shape are loaded.

## Test Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD`
- `node -e "await import('./src/Neo.mjs'); await import('./src/core/_export.mjs'); const {listTools}=await import('./ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/toolService.mjs'); const names=listTools({}).tools.map(t=>t.name); if (!names.includes('list_agent_faqs')) throw new Error('list_agent_faqs missing'); console.log(names.filter(n=>n.includes('faq')).join(','));"` -> `list_agent_faqs`
- `npx playwright test -c test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/KBRecorderService.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/DreamService.spec.mjs` -> 17 passed

## Commit

- `b86c9301c` — `feat(ai): capture KB query telemetry FAQs (#10081)`

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Active clones update gitignored Knowledge Base `config.mjs` shape and restart KB MCP / daemon processes.
- [ ] Run `npm run ai:build-kb-faqs -- --min-count 3 --limit 100` after enough KB traffic exists to verify production FAQ materialization volume.

> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves [#10081](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10081)
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** Epic #10030 (Concept Ontology — parent), #10037 ChromaDB Concept Embedding (just merged earlier today; future embedding-backed clustering substrate), Neural Link RecorderService (sibling pattern), GapInferenceEngine `[KB_DEMAND_GAP]` lineage from #10086/#10087/#10100/#10101
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenges (per guide §7.1):** five non-blocking observations:
> 
> 1. **`memoryCoreDbPath` default uses `os.homedir()` not `neoRootDir`.** Line 116 of `config.template.mjs` defaults to `path.join(os.homedir(), '.neo-ai-data', 'memory-core.sqlite')`, but the existing Chroma `path` (line 110) uses `path.resolve(neoRootDir, '.neo-ai-data/chroma/knowledge-base')` — repo-relative. Different conventions in the same config block. Could be intentional (cross-clone unification via home dir) or an oversight. Worth a one-line clarification in the JSDoc explaining why home-dir is the right scope here. Non-blocking — your `process.env.NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` override gives operators the escape hatch — but the convention divergence will surface as a post-merge follow-up question if not documented.
> 
> 2. **`kb_query_log` table is unbounded.** No DELETE / retention policy. Heartbeat cadence × N agents will accumulate rows continuously. Not a Phase 0 blocker, but worth a follow-up ticket: "KB telemetry retention policy — keep last N days, archive older to compressed JSONL." The Neural Link `nl_action_log` may have the same gap — if so, file as cross-substrate adjacent debt.
> 
> 3. **`buildAgentFaqs()` reads ALL `kb_query_log` rows since `sinceTimestamp` with no upper bound or pagination.** Line 251's `WHERE timestamp >= ?` could OOM on a large log. Phase 0 mitigation: add a hard-limit (e.g., max 100k rows) with a `logger.warn` if reached, so operators see the signal before SQLite gets crushed. Worth tracking even if low-probability for current traffic.
> 
> 4. **`hasStrongGuideCoverage()` calls `ConceptService.getConceptCoverage()` — verify it exists.** Line 248 of `KBRecorderService.mjs` calls `ConceptService.getConceptCoverage(conceptId).explainedBy.length > 0`. The try/catch returns false on error, so a missing method silently degrades to "no coverage detected" (which is the safe default). But if the method doesn't exist, ALL FAQs would always have `hasStrongGuideCoverage: false`, defeating the gating logic. Quick verification: did you confirm `ConceptService.getConceptCoverage` is exported and returns the expected shape? If it's a new method we're adding via this PR, the diff should include it.
> 
> 5. **OpenAPI tool description budget per §5.3.** `list_agent_faqs` description is multi-line block-literal (`|`) with 6 lines covering source tables + refresh:true semantics + similarity-threshold calibration framing. The architectural narrative around "calibration value for the later embedding-backed clustering phase" arguably belongs in JSDoc on `KBRecorderService.listAgentFaqs()` (the method already has it), not the agent-facing OpenAPI surface. Could tighten to: *"Lists frequently repeated KB questions captured from agent calls. Set `refresh: true` to rebuild from raw query log before reading. Cluster grouping is exact normalized-query (Phase 0)."* — single line, usage-focused, drops the calibration-history narrative. Non-blocking; the multi-line form is acceptable here per §5.3 ("genuinely complex contracts") but worth tightening if you agree.
>
